summary:
Ultrasound has a legitimate clinical role in visualizing dynamic motion and soft tissue abnormalities linked to internal derangement of the temporomandibular joint. However, it lacks consistent reliability in accurately determining disc position and other pathologies essential for diagnosis, making it less effective than MRI.  

\boxed{B}
